items.descriptionZh: 春节是中国最重要的传统节日，家人团聚一起过年。
items.descriptionEn: Chinese New Year is the most important traditional festival, when families gather together.

items.descriptionZh: 长辈把压岁钱装进红包送给小朋友，祝福平安健康。
items.descriptionEn: Elders give children lucky money in red envelopes for good fortune.

items.descriptionZh: 红纸上写着吉祥话，贴在门两边迎接新年。
items.descriptionEn: Lucky words written on red paper are pasted beside doors to welcome the new year.

items.descriptionZh: 北方人过年吃饺子，形状像小元宝，代表福气。
items.descriptionEn: Dumplings look like little gold ingots and stand for good luck at New Year.

items.descriptionZh: 除夕晚上全家人一起吃的团圆大餐。
items.descriptionEn: The big family dinner on New Year's Eve when everyone reunites.

items.descriptionZh: 锣鼓声中狮子跳起舞，热热闹闹庆新年。
items.descriptionEn: Lions dance to drums and gongs to celebrate the new year.

items.descriptionZh: 红红的灯笼挂起来，节日的夜晚亮堂堂。
items.descriptionEn: Red lanterns light up the festival nights.

items.descriptionZh: 五彩的烟花在夜空绽放，像盛开的花朵。
items.descriptionEn: Colorful fireworks bloom in the night sky like flowers.

items.descriptionZh: 见到长辈说一声「新年好」，送上新年的祝福。
items.descriptionEn: Say Happy New Year to elders and share good wishes.

items.descriptionZh: 十二生肖轮流值年，每个小朋友都有自己的属相。
items.descriptionEn: Twelve zodiac animals take turns each year; every child has one.

items.descriptionZh: 圆圆的汤圆甜又糯，象征一家人团团圆圆。
items.descriptionEn: Round sweet rice balls stand for family togetherness.

items.descriptionZh: 一年的最后一个晚上，全家守岁迎接新年。
items.descriptionEn: On the last night of the year, families stay up to welcome the new year.
